pattern: Each query asks for the k-th smallest by a suffix of fixed-length digit strings, which sorts stably on (trimmed-suffix, original-index) so equal trims break ties by lower index.
hints: Trimming to the rightmost t digits of equal-length numeric strings is just comparing their last t characters lexicographically.
For one query, pair each number's suffix with its index and pick the k-th in sorted order, with ties broken by the smaller index.
Answer each query independently: sort the indices by (number[i][-t:], i) and read off the (k-1)-th index.

edge_inputs.description: trim to one digit, smallest wins
trim equals full length leaves strings unchanged
equal trimmed values break ties by lower original index
all identical strings, every query returns the first index

stress.description: 100 random equal-length digit strings with many queries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    L = rng.randint(1, 6)
    nums = [''.join(str(rng.randint(0, 9)) for _ in range(L)) for _ in range(n)]
    q = rng.randint(1, 100)
    queries = [[rng.randint(1, n), rng.randint(1, L)] for _ in range(q)]
    return [nums, queries]
stress.note: exercises ties and varied trim lengths
